step_by_step_thinking:
Antibody-dependent enhancement (ADE) refers specifically to situations where pre-existing immunity actually enhances rather than protects against subsequent viral reinfection. For dengue fever, particularly upon secondary exposure to a different dengue virus serotype, there is strong evidence indicating that prior immune responses can exacerbate illness due to the presence of antibodies that do not fully neutralize the new strain, thereby facilitating entry into cells and increasing viremia – thus fitting the description of ADE.
\boxed{D} Based on the evidence showing how previous immune responses to one dengue virus serotype enhance susceptibility and severity to another serotype, it strongly implicates antibody-dependent enhancement in dengue hemorrhagic fever.
